name: Think Python: How to Think Like a Computer Scientist

metadata.value: Think Python: How to Think Like a Computer Scientist

Think Python is an introduction to Python programming for beginners. It starts with basic concepts of programming, and is carefully designed to define all terms when they are first used and to develop each new concept in a logical progression.

name: Python for Everybody: Exploring Data Using Python 3

metadata.value: Python for Everybody: Exploring Data Using Python 3

This book is designed to introduce students to programming and computational thinking through the lens of exploring data. You can think of Python as your tool to solve data problems that are beyond the capability of a spreadsheet.
This book introduces the core libraries essential for working with data in Python: particularly IPython, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Scikit-Learn, and related packages.

name: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python

metadata.value: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python
A practical programming guide for office workers, academics, and anyone who wants to save time and improve productivity by automating tasks on their computers using Python.

name: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science

metadata.value: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science
This book is designed to be used as the primary textbook in a college-level first course in computing. The focus is on teaching Python programming fundamentals through hands-on problem solving.

name: Introduction to Scientific Programming with Python

metadata.value: Introduction to Scientific Programming with Python
This open-access textbook introduces scientific programming using Python, covering numerical methods, data structures, and visualization tools used in scientific computing.
A comprehensive introductory programming textbook using Python as the teaching language, covering algorithms, data structures, object-oriented programming, and software engineering principles.
An accessible and comprehensive guide to machine learning with Python and scikit-learn, covering supervised and unsupervised learning, neural networks, and deep learning.

name: Computational and Inferential Thinking: The Foundations of Data Science

metadata.value: Computational and Inferential Thinking: The Foundations of Data Science
The textbook for the Foundations of Data Science course at UC Berkeley, covering Python programming, data visualization, statistical inference, and prediction.
A hands-on guide to Python 3 for experienced programmers, covering native datatypes, comprehensions, generators, classes, iterators, advanced iterators, unit testing, and more.
An introduction to Python programming oriented towards bioinformatics and computational biology applications, covering sequence analysis, data parsing, and biological data visualization.
A practical guide to scientific computing using Python, NumPy, SciPy, and Matplotlib, covering numerical methods, linear algebra, optimization, and differential equations.
A practical guide to extracting data from websites using Python, covering HTML parsing, CSS selectors, XPath, browser automation, and handling JavaScript-rendered content.
A guide to natural language processing with Python and NLTK, covering tokenization, part-of-speech tagging, parsing, sentiment analysis, and machine translation.
A comprehensive guide to building geospatial applications with Python, covering GIS concepts, spatial databases, map rendering, and geospatial analysis.

name: Problem Solving with Algorithms and Data Structures Using Python

metadata.value: Problem Solving with Algorithms and Data Structures Using Python
An interactive textbook introducing computer science and programming in Python, with a focus on problem solving, algorithms, data structures, and abstract data types.
A comprehensive introduction to Python programming covering variables, expressions, conditional statements, loops, functions, recursion, lists, and object-oriented programming.
An introduction to Python for finance professionals, covering financial data analysis, algorithmic trading, derivatives analytics, and asset management with pandas, NumPy, and SciPy.

name: Coding the Matrix: Linear Algebra through Computer Science Applications

metadata.value: Coding the Matrix: Linear Algebra through Computer Science Applications
An introduction to linear algebra through computer science applications using Python, covering vectors, matrices, linear transformations, and applications in image processing and machine learning.
Recipes for mastering Python 3, covering data structures, algorithms, text processing, iterators, generators, metaprogramming, network programming, and concurrency.

name: Introduction to Programming in Python: An Interdisciplinary Approach

metadata.value: Introduction to Programming in Python: An Interdisciplinary Approach
A Python programming textbook for students in science, mathematics, and engineering using an interdisciplinary approach that emphasizes applications in a variety of fields.
An introduction to deep learning with Python, covering neural networks, convolutional networks, recurrent networks, generative adversarial networks, and deployment.
A practical guide to building language-aware data products using Python, covering text classification, entity extraction, topic modeling, and building machine learning pipelines.

name: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow

metadata.value: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
A practical guide to machine learning with Python using scikit-learn, Keras, and TensorFlow, covering supervised and unsupervised learning, neural networks, and production deployment.

name: Python and HDF5: Unlocking Scientific Data

metadata.value: Python and HDF5: Unlocking Scientific Data
A practical introduction to managing large scientific datasets with Python and HDF5, covering the h5py library, parallel I/O, file organization, and integration with NumPy.

name: Introduction to Computation and Programming Using Python

metadata.value: Introduction to Computation and Programming Using Python
An MIT textbook introducing computational thinking and programming using Python, with emphasis on algorithm design, software engineering, and data science.
An introduction to scientific programming in Python, covering NumPy, SciPy, Matplotlib, and specialized tools for physics, chemistry, and engineering applications.

name: Data Science from Scratch: First Principles with Python

metadata.value: Data Science from Scratch: First Principles with Python
An introduction to the math and hacking skills needed to practice data science from scratch using Python, covering linear algebra, statistics, probability, machine learning, and data wrangling.
A practical guide to pytest, the popular Python testing framework, covering test organization, fixtures, parametrize, plugins, and integration with CI/CD pipelines.
An introduction to Python and its scientific ecosystem for astronomers, covering data analysis, visualization, statistical methods, and common astronomical data formats.
A practical guide to data analysis with Python and pandas, covering data wrangling, time series analysis, visualization, and building machine learning pipelines.
A guide to writing effective, idiomatic Python code, covering data structures, functions, object-oriented idioms, control flow, and metaprogramming.
A practical guide to design patterns for Python developers, covering creational, structural, and behavioral patterns with Pythonic implementations.

name: Building Machine Learning Systems with Python

metadata.value: Building Machine Learning Systems with Python
A practical guide to building machine learning systems using Python and scikit-learn, covering classification, regression, clustering, recommendation systems, and text analysis.

name: Python for Computational Science and Engineering

metadata.value: Python for Computational Science and Engineering
An open-access textbook introducing Python for scientists and engineers, covering the scientific Python ecosystem including NumPy, SciPy, matplotlib, and pandas.
A comprehensive guide to using Python for scientific computing, covering scripting, numerical computing, visualization, software engineering, and high-performance computing.

name: A Primer on Scientific Programming with Python

metadata.value: A Primer on Scientific Programming with Python
A textbook for scientists and engineers to learn Python programming, emphasizing good programming habits and covering computational mathematics and differential equations.

name: Python Algorithms: Mastering Basic Algorithms in the Python Language

metadata.value: Python Algorithms: Mastering Basic Algorithms in the Python Language
A comprehensive guide to understanding and implementing algorithms in Python, covering data structures, sorting, searching, graphs, dynamic programming, and NP-completeness.
A practical guide to parallel and concurrent programming in Python, covering threads, processes, async I/O, distributed computing, and GPU programming.
A comprehensive guide to network programming with Python, covering TCP/IP, UDP, sockets, HTTP, REST APIs, email, FTP, and network security.
The definitive guide to data analysis using Python with pandas, including data manipulation, time series analysis, file I/O, data visualization, and tips for financial data analysis.
A comprehensive guide to object-oriented programming in Python, covering classes, inheritance, polymorphism, abstract classes, design patterns, and testing.
An introduction to Python programming for social science researchers, covering data collection, cleaning, analysis, visualization, and reproducible research practices.
An introduction to Python for economists and econometricians, covering linear regression, time series analysis, panel data, and financial econometrics using statsmodels and pandas.

name: Numerical Python: Scientific Computing and Data Science Applications with NumPy, SciPy, and Matplotlib

metadata.value: Numerical Python: Scientific Computing and Data Science Applications with NumPy, SciPy, and Matplotlib
A comprehensive guide to numerical computing with Python using NumPy, SciPy, and Matplotlib, covering linear algebra, optimization, statistics, signal processing, and data visualization.
A practical guide to building microservices with Python, covering service design, REST APIs with Flask/FastAPI, asynchronous messaging, containers, and deployment.
A practical guide to Python performance optimization, covering profiling, memory management, NumPy, Cython, multiprocessing, asyncio, and GPU computing.
A practical guide to Python for DevOps and system administration, covering automation, CI/CD, containers, Kubernetes, monitoring, and machine learning operations.
A comprehensive guide to using regular expressions in Python for pattern matching, text extraction, and data validation, covering the re module and advanced techniques.
A guide to security programming with Python, covering cryptography, network security, secure coding practices, vulnerability assessment, and building security tools.
